documents.title: Birth Certificate (Original / Duplicate)
documents.description: Official record of birth issued by Municipal Corporation, Gram Panchayat, or Civil Registration System. Required for school admissions, passport, Aadhaar, government jobs.

required_documents.category: For registering a NEW birth (within 21 days)
options: Hospital discharge slip (if hospital birth) — hospital files report directly
For home births: Informant's identity proof (parent Aadhaar/Voter ID)
Parent's marriage certificate
Parents' identity proofs

required_documents.category: For DELAYED registration (after 21 days, within 1 year) — additional documents
options: Affidavit from parent/guardian
School leaving/transfer certificate (if child is school-going)
Verification certificate from local official (village headman, ward officer)

required_documents.category: For DUPLICATE / certified copy of existing certificate
options: Application form from Municipal/Panchayat office
Proof of identity of applicant (Aadhaar / Voter ID)
Details of original registration: registration number, date of birth, place of birth
Relationship proof (if applying on behalf of another person)

process_steps.title: Identify the correct issuing authority
process_steps.detail: Urban area (town/city): Contact Municipal Corporation / Municipality Birth & Death Registration office. Rural area: Contact Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office (BDO). Hospitals: Larger hospitals have their own registration desk.
process_steps.tip: The authority covers the area where the birth actually occurred, not where you currently live.

process_steps.title: Search for existing registration
process_steps.detail: If birth was registered earlier, ask for the Registration Number. Many states now have online search: visit your state civil registration portal. Maharashtra: aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in | Karnataka: sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in | Tamil Nadu: tnreginet.gov.in | Telangana: crsorgi.gov.in
process_steps.tip: If you know the hospital, they usually have records even if you don't have the registration number.

process_steps.title: Apply online (where available) or visit office
process_steps.detail: Online: Go to your state's Civil Registration portal → Search/apply for birth certificate → Fill form with date/place/parents' names → Pay fee → Download or collect certificate. Offline: Visit the municipal/panchayat office with identity proof and fill prescribed application form.
process_steps.tip: Online portals are available in Maharashtra, Karnataka, Delhi, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, AP, Rajasthan, UP, and others.

process_steps.title: For late/delayed registration (birth not yet registered)
process_steps.detail: Visit BDO/Municipal office → Submit affidavit + supporting documents → Application reviewed by Medical Officer or Revenue Officer → If approved, registration is done and certificate issued. Process takes 30–60 days.
process_steps.tip: A notarised affidavit from parent/guardian stating the birth details is the most important document for delayed registration.

process_steps.title: For very old births (pre-1970) — special process
process_steps.detail: Records may be in district registrar archives. Visit District Registrar's office with approximate birth details. Some states have digitised old records; check your state portal. If not found, you may need to get a Non-Availability Certificate (NAC) from the Registrar, which is accepted as substitute proof.
process_steps.tip: Non-Availability Certificate (NAC) is legally accepted for Aadhaar, passport, and most government purposes in place of birth certificate.

process_steps.title: Collect and verify certificate
process_steps.detail: Certificate shows: child's name, parents' names, date/time/place of birth, registration number, registrar's signature and seal. Verify all spellings carefully.
process_steps.tip: If any detail is wrong, apply for correction immediately — corrections require affidavit and are harder to do years later.

documents.fees: ₹0–50 for first copy | ₹20–100 for duplicate (varies by state)
documents.processing_time: Same day to 7 days (online) | 7–30 days (offline/delayed registration)
documents.helpline: Vary by state — check your state Civil Registration portal

documents.title: Marriage Certificate
documents.description: Official registration of marriage under Hindu Marriage Act 1955 or Special Marriage Act 1954. Required for spouse visa, passport name change, joint property, and bank accounts.

required_documents.category: Both bride and groom must provide
options: Age proof (Birth Certificate / Matriculation Certificate / Passport) — groom must be 21+, bride must be 18+
Address proof (Aadhaar / Voter ID / Passport / Driving Licence)
Passport-size photographs (4 each, usually)
Marriage photograph (if registering after ceremony)
Invitation card of the marriage (if already solemnised)
Two witnesses with their Aadhaar/Voter ID (must be present at office)
Priest/Pandit certificate (for Hindu marriages)
Affidavit by both parties stating date, place, marital status (not previously married or if widowed/divorced, proof of same)
Death certificate of deceased spouse (if widow/widower)
Divorce decree from court (if divorced)

process_steps.title: Choose the applicable law and office
process_steps.detail: Hindu Marriage Act: For Hindus, Buddhists, Jains, Sikhs — register at Sub-Registrar's office in the area where marriage took place or either party resides. Special Marriage Act: For inter-religion marriages or any couple — register at Marriage Registration Office (Sub-Divisional Magistrate's office). Court Marriage = Special Marriage Act.
process_steps.tip: Special Marriage Act requires 30-day notice period before solemnisation.

process_steps.title: Online appointment booking (in most cities)
process_steps.detail: Most states now have online portals. Maharashtra: aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in | Delhi: edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in | Karnataka: sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in. Book appointment, fill form online, and pay fee.
process_steps.tip: Bring all originals + one set of self-attested photocopies.

process_steps.title: Visit office with both parties + 2 witnesses
process_steps.detail: Both bride and groom must be physically present. Two witnesses (any adult Indian citizens) must also be present with their photo ID. Submit documents, sign the register.
process_steps.tip: Marriage certificate is usually issued the same day for Hindu Marriage Act registrations.

process_steps.title: Special Marriage Act — 30-day notice
process_steps.detail: File notice at SDM office → Notice is displayed publicly for 30 days → If no objections, marriage is solemnised at the office → Certificate issued same day.
process_steps.tip: During the 30-day notice period, anyone can raise an objection. This is a legal requirement and cannot be bypassed.

documents.processing_time: Same day (Hindu Marriage Act) | 30+ days (Special Marriage Act)
documents.website: Varies by state — check your state's revenue/registration portal

documents.title: Death Certificate
documents.description: Official record of death issued by Municipal Authority or Gram Panchayat. Required for insurance claims, property inheritance, pension, bank account closure.

options: Hospital death summary / Medical cause of death certificate (if hospital death)
For home deaths: Informant's identity proof (family member's Aadhaar/Voter ID)
Identity proof of the deceased (any government ID)
Address proof of the deceased
Application form from Municipal/Panchayat office
Applicant's identity proof
Relationship proof with deceased
Registration number / details of original registration

process_steps.title: Register within 21 days of death
process_steps.detail: Hospital deaths: Hospital files report directly — you only need to collect the certificate from the hospital or municipal office. Home deaths: Inform the local authority (municipal ward office or Gram Panchayat) within 21 days.
process_steps.tip: Cremation/burial grounds in urban areas also report deaths to the municipality.

process_steps.title: Apply for certificate online or at municipal office
process_steps.detail: Use your state's civil registration portal (same as birth certificate portals) or visit the municipal office with required documents. Fill application form, submit documents, pay fee.

process_steps.title: For delayed registration (after 21 days)
process_steps.detail: Attach affidavit from family member + any supporting proof of death (hospital records, religious ceremony records, witness statements). Approval required from Medical Officer or local magistrate.
process_steps.tip: Police report is sometimes asked for unexplained or sudden deaths.
